title: potentilla douglasii var. tenella
intro: Potentilla douglasii var. tenella, also known as creeping cinquefoil, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with yellow flowers, native to the western coastal regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Potentilla douglasii var. tenella include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, reduce fever, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
